description_long: This object is a sculpted stone resembling a lizard or iguana resting on a roughly hewn rock base. The stone is light gray with darker speckles, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The lizard's body is elongated, with distinct limbs and a long tail that follows the contours of the base. The surface is uneven and textured, with visible chisel marks and irregularities that suggest hand-carving. The stone's coloration and rough finish evoke the look of granite or similar durable rock. This type of sculptural stone is often used as a decorative feature in parks, gardens, or urban plazas.
description: A hand-carved, speckled gray stone sculpture of a lizard resting on a rough rock base, with detailed limbs and a long tail.
description_view_0: The lizard's head and upper body are clearly visible, with the front limbs and facial features most prominent.
description_view_1: The elongated tail and back of the lizard are emphasized, showing the full length of the sculpture.
description_view_2: The side profile highlights the lizard's body pressed against the rock, with the head slightly raised.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the lizard's face and chest, with the mouth and front limbs most visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground or atop a low wall
attribute_pattern: Natural speckling and mottling with irregular chisel marks; no artificial carvings beyond the sculpted lizard form.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly eroded, with visible surface texture indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with stone or gravel landscaping, often surrounded by plants or set among other decorative rocks.
